paper_metadata.title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
paper_metadata.abstract: We introduce LLaMA, a collection of foundation language models ranging from 7B to 65B parameters. We train our models on trillions of tokens, and show that it is possible to train state-of-the-art models using publicly available datasets exclusively, without resorting to proprietary and inaccessible datasets. In particular, LLaMA-13B outperforms GPT-3 (175B) on most benchmarks, and LLaMA-65B is competitive with the best models, Chinchilla-70B and PaLM-540B. We release all our models to the research community.

extraction_data.innovation: Efficient scaling of language models using publicly available datasets, achieving competitive performance with smaller models.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large, diverse corpus of text including Common Crawl, Wikipedia, and books
extraction_data.extension: Efficient scaling of model parameters for better performance and accessibility
extraction_data.research_problem: Development of large language models for natural language understanding and generation.
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, question answering, text generation, code generation
extraction_data.paper_title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models

extraction_data.innovation: Efficient and open foundation language models using Transformer decoder architecture
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale text corpus (internet, books, and user-generated content)
extraction_data.application: General-purpose language modeling, text generation, summarization, question answering
